_meta.name: timing
_meta.purpose: Claude classifies as timing — the element exists but wasn't ready when the step ran. Suggests @Delay/@Timeout, not a selector rebind.
_meta.matches_on: prompt contains 'wait timeout exceeded' (from error_message in DiagnoseInput)

output.human_explanation: The dashboard widget renders 1.5-3s after page load (async fetch). The default 1s wait inside the assertion fires before the DOM stabilises. This is a timing race, not a selector or app issue — the same step passes on a retried run ~60% of the time.
evidence: TimeoutError at locator.waitFor — element appeared 1.8s after the wait window closed (per trace.zip)
Sibling scenarios bound the same element successfully when preceded by a 2s page-ready check
Telemetry: same step failed in 3 of the last 10 runs (intermittent — not consistent)

alternatives_considered.rejected_because: The selector resolves correctly on the retry — the element exists, only the timing was wrong
Other scenarios on the same page passed; this is isolated to the async widget
